title: The space industry is weighing ambitious hiring against heritage
summary: The space industry is balancing the need for ambitious hiring with the value of heritage expertise. The challenge of 'rocket science' as a barrier to entry is being re-evaluated amid evolving industry demands.

title: Startup testing nuclear battery technology in orbit
summary: A City Labs mission is testing a tritium-powered nuclear battery in orbit to validate its use for satellites and lunar systems in environments where solar power is insufficient. The technology could enable long-duration operations beyond Earth's orbit.

title: Space capitalism needs more than a bull market
summary: Commercial space industry is thriving with recent policy activity in Washington, including executive orders and directives, but requires more than just market growth to succeed.

title: ElevationSpace advances work on commercial reentry vehicle
summary: ElevationSpace, a Japanese startup, is progressing on its commercial reentry vehicle while signing up customers and preparing for its first mission. The company is closely monitoring SpaceX's entry into the reentry vehicle market.

title: China becomes second country to recover orbital booster with Long March 10B
summary: China successfully launched its Long March 10B rocket and recovered the first stage, becoming the second country to achieve orbital booster recovery. This marks a significant advancement in China's reusable rocket technology.
company: China (CASC - China Aerospace Science and Technology Corporation)

title: Interior Department requests information on offshore launch options
summary: The U.S. Department of the Interior is seeking information on concepts for offshore orbital launches to reduce congestion at existing spaceports. This effort is part of a broader initiative to explore alternative launch options.

title: SDA TAP Lab Evolves Into BMC3I TAP Lab And In Partnership With Catalyst Campus Launches A New Multi-Phased Program To Accelerate Mission-Focused Technology Development
summary: The SDA TAP Lab has been rebranded as the BMC3I TAP Lab and, in partnership with Catalyst Campus, launched a new multi-phased program to accelerate mission-focused technology development. This initiative aims to enhance collaboration between commercial innovators and government entities.
company: SDA (Space Development Agency) and Catalyst Campus for Technology and Innovation

title: The Exploration Company establishes US entity to pursue government contracts
summary: The Exploration Company, a German space transportation firm, has set up a US-based entity named TEC Federal to compete for government contracts. This move aims to expand its opportunities in the American space sector.

title: Environmental groups urge FCC to pause orbital data center applications
summary: Environmental and scientific groups are urging the FCC to pause applications for orbital data centers, citing concerns over the environmental impact and lack of oversight. The push aligns with broader demands for stricter regulations on megaconstellations.

title: Xona unveils program to verify Pulsar-compatible devices
summary: Xona, a Silicon Valley startup, announced a program to verify devices compatible with its Pulsar precision positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) constellation. The company revealed a high-profile roster of partners developing compatible equipment.

title: SpaceX launch creates colorful 'jellyfish' in the night sky | Space photo of the day for July 10, 2026
summary: A SpaceX launch created a visually striking 'jellyfish' effect in the night sky, captured in a space photo. The phenomenon was later confirmed to be unrelated to extraterrestrial activity.

title: Supreme Court ruling on mail-in ballots ensures astronauts can vote from space  — or anywhere else
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court ruling on mail-in ballots may allow astronauts to vote from space or anywhere else. This decision could extend voting rights to astronauts training internationally.

title: Astronomers may have heard the 1st 'whispers' of ghost particles created by supernova explosions
summary: Astronomers may have detected the first faint signals of neutrinos, often called 'ghost particles,' which are remnants of ancient supernova explosions. These elusive particles could provide insights into stellar deaths that occurred billions of years ago.

title: Don't miss out on 'The Ark' Season 3 — get a year-long Peacock TV deal for just over $9 a month
summary: Peacock TV is offering a year-long subscription deal for just over $9 a month to watch 'The Ark' Season 3, a sci-fi series set in outer space. The promotion highlights the platform's expanding sci-fi content library.

title: Making history! China lands rocket during an orbital launch for 1st time ever (video)
summary: China successfully conducted its first orbital launch with a Long March 10B rocket, deploying a satellite and achieving a historic sea landing of the rocket. This marks a significant milestone in China's spaceflight capabilities.

title: Wally Funk, trailblazing pilot and astronaut, passes away at 87
summary: Wally Funk, a pioneering pilot and astronaut who flew to space at 82, has passed away at age 87. She was a lifelong advocate for women in aviation and aerospace.

title: Scientists have discovered the oldest quasar ever seen, and it shines with the light of a trillion suns
summary: Scientists discovered the oldest quasar ever observed, shining with the brightness of a trillion suns. The finding was made using the ESA's Euclid space telescope, which revealed a wealth of black hole-powered quasars from the early universe.

title: SpaceX wants to launch 100,000 Starlink satellites to orbit
summary: SpaceX has applied for approval to launch a megaconstellation of 100,000 next-generation Starlink satellites, each weighing approximately 4,400 pounds.

title: 'Reckless' space-based data centers lack environmental review, drawing criticism
summary: Critics warn that the proliferation of space-based data centers without environmental reviews poses significant risks. The lack of oversight has been deemed irresponsible and reckless by industry observers.

title: Where Venezuela’s Earthquakes Shifted the Ground
summary: Radar data from the NISAR satellite revealed significant ground displacement in La Guaira and nearby areas due to Venezuela's June 2026 earthquakes. This highlights the satellite's capability to monitor seismic activity and its impacts.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon is set to launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on July 14, joining Expedition 74 crew members for scientific research. The mission will be accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina.

title: NASA Space Telescope Maps Magnetic Fields of ‘Lighthouse’ Pulsar
summary: NASA's IXPE has directly measured the magnetic fields of the pulsar PSR J1101−6101 within the Lighthouse Nebula for the first time, offering new insights into extreme cosmic objects.

title: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports Evaluate Umbra Synthetic Aperture Radar Data
summary: Reports evaluate Umbra's Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research. This adds to the growing documentation on commercial data's role in scientific applications.

title: Curiosity Sees Martian Sulfur Up Close
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover discovered fragments of sulfur crystals on the Red Planet after accidentally crushing a rock on May 30, 2024. This marks the first time such crystals have been observed on Mars.

title: NASA Scientists Take to Air and Space to Study Arctic Sea Ice
summary: NASA engineers are testing a spacecraft sensor to measure Arctic sea ice loss, with field research conducted in the Canadian wilderness ahead of its planned launch next year.

title: Super Typhoon Bavi
summary: Super Typhoon Bavi, the third Category 5 tropical cyclone of 2026, impacted the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam before heading toward Asia. The storm posed significant risks to space industry operations in the region.

title: Students Connect NASA Science With Indigenous Knowledge to Study Coastal Erosion
summary: NASA science is being combined with Indigenous knowledge to study coastal erosion affecting the Pleasant Point Passamaquoddy Reservation. The project aims to address rapid shoreline changes that threaten the community's land and heritage.

title: Hubble Captures Star-Studded Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of Messier 3 (M3), a massive globular cluster in the Milky Way galaxy. The cluster consists of ancient stars formed from the same gas cloud, giving them similar ages.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, marking a continued revitalization of the region. Multiple launch providers are advancing their facilities at Cape Canaveral.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital destination ahead of its deployment.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is advancing preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are accelerating efforts to meet this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar missions and the establishment of a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral following a recent New Glenn anomaly that caused significant damage. The company is employing a hybrid plan to restore the facility.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021. The mission aims to extend the operational life of the Swift space telescope.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help scientists study the galaxy's structure and dynamics.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry-related topic, but the specific details were not provided in the given input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm technology that aims to dominate the space industry. This innovation could significantly impact satellite servicing and in-space assembly.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a significant step forward for the mission's scientific objectives.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft using innovative approaches from young researchers. This ensures the durability of ESA's missions in the harsh space environment.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The article discusses the importance of sensitivity and sturdiness in space industry components. It highlights the need for durable and precise materials in spacecraft design.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for beaming power to satellites in orbit. The funding will support the advancement of their laser systems designed for in-space power transmission.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The U.S. government is exploring offshore launch opportunities by studying the potential for building or repurposing infrastructure on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launches and reentries. The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather relevant data.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies announced plans to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective space-based defense capabilities.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, marking a shift away from Jeff Bezos' personal funding. The move signals the company's transition toward external investment.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology in orbit. This achievement highlights potential future applications for sustainable bioprinting in space.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for lunar missions, with launches planned no earlier than 2030. This initiative marks a new lunar integrator service offering for commercial and scientific payloads.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million in a Series B funding round to scale production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. The investment highlights growing industry interest in RDRE, a more efficient rocket propulsion method.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing processes. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the upcoming decision, which could modernize regulations for the space industry.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to Low Earth Orbit (LEO), including the first commercial nuclear satellite. The mission lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base at 3:12 AM.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA is advancing its plan to support standalone replacements for the ISS by releasing a draft RFP for the Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLD) program. The move follows industry demonstrations of human habitation capabilities in low Earth orbit.

title: MDA Space Vertically Integrates in EO Analytics With Deal to Acquire Majority of CLS
summary: MDA Space is acquiring a majority stake in French Earth observation analytics company CLS to expand its vertical integration in EO analytics. The deal aims to enhance MDA Space's capabilities in data processing and insights from satellite observations.

title: SpaceX Files for 100,000-Satellite Gen-3 Constellation
summary: SpaceX has filed with the FCC to launch a Gen3 satellite constellation consisting of 100,000 satellites, aiming to provide multi-gigabit connectivity. The filing includes plans to utilize W- and D-band frequencies.

title: Telesat to Provide Mil-Ka Arctic Connectivity for Canada’s ESCP-P Program
summary: Telesat has secured a deal with the Canadian government to supply Military Ka-band satellite capacity for Canada's Enhanced Satellite Communications Project – Polar (ESCP-P) program. The agreement aims to enhance Arctic connectivity for military and government use.

title: Rivada Signs Connectivity Partner in Nepal
summary: Rivada Space Networks has partnered with a telecoms company in Nepal to provide connectivity services. The deal involves Mercantile Space, a communications technology firm in the country with a population of around 30 million.

title: Impulse Space and Relativity Space Join the Field for Space Force NSSL Phase 3, Lane 1
summary: Impulse Space and Relativity Space have joined the list of eligible companies for the U.S. Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3, Lane 1 awards. The field now includes seven companies after Space Systems Command's announcement.

title: Beyond Gravity Signs Gamechanging Contract with Ariane Group
summary: Beyond Gravity has signed a major contract with ArianeGroup, solidifying its role in the European launch supply chain. This deal is one of the largest in Beyond Gravity's history.

title: Iceye Makes Major Move in German With New Business Entity
summary: Iceye has established a new business entity in Germany, based in Berlin and Munich, to expand its presence in the German space market. This move marks Iceye as the latest major player entering the dynamic German space industry.

title: York Space and All.Space CEOs Detail Resilient Communications Vision as Acquisition Closes
summary: York Space Systems completed its acquisition of satcom terminal company All.Space to create a complete tactical satcom ecosystem. Both CEOs emphasized the vision for resilient communications in the deal.

title: CesiumAstro Files for 737-Satellite Synchronicity Constellation
summary: CesiumAstro has filed an FCC application for a 737-satellite constellation named Synchronicity to offer reconfigurable connectivity for fixed and mobile satellite users. The company, based in Austin, Texas, specializes in software-defined radio technology.

title: NATO Allies Plan Hybrid HALO Constellation
summary: Eight NATO allies are collaborating to create a hybrid military satellite constellation named HALO to enhance NATO's space-based resilience and military capabilities. The initiative aims to integrate national military satellites into a unified system.
